title: pocketdimensions:pocketdimensions-handbook-title-guide
text: Pocket Dimensions creates small persistent pocket spaces backed by DimensionLib. A pocket can have multiple vertical layers, a generated floor, a return pedestal, a central Pocket Elevator, and optional external Pocket Waystones. See DimensionLib Basics for the shared infrastructure concepts. Pocket Directory - Press Ctrl+Shift+P to open the Pocket Directory. - The directory lists pockets and layers your server permissions allow you to see. - The current layer is marked as Current. Other available layers show an Enter button. - If your server allows pocket creation, use New Pocket to choose a display name, slug, size, and spawn Y. - If your server allows layer creation, select a pocket and use New Layer. Choose an index adjacent to an existing layer, such as +1 or -1. - If your server allows metadata edits, use Edit beside a layer to rename it. Submit a blank name to reset that layer to its default name. Travel - Use the directory's Enter button to teleport to an available layer without typing a command. - Right-clicking a bound external Pocket Waystone enters that pocket and makes the return pedestal send you back to that Waystone. - Stand on a Pocket Elevator and press PageUp or PageDown to move to an adjacent layer when your server allows it. - The Pocket Return Pedestal returns you to your active Waystone entry, your directory/command entry point, or a safe fallback. Fallback commands - /pocket create <name> [sizeChunks] [spawnY] creates a pocket when you have permission. - /pocket enter <name> enters a pocket. - /pocket exit returns from the current pocket. - /pocket list lists registered pockets. - /pocket layers lists layer stacks for debugging or recovery.
